arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames. I can see the tombstone has frames from:
- `libc.so` (Android system)
- `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (Mono runtime - .NET runtime)
- `libSystem.Native.so` (.NET native library)
- `libSystem.Globalization.Native.so` (.NET native library)

Each has a BuildId. To symbolicate these, I need to:
1. Download the debug symbols for these libraries using their BuildIds
2. Use tools like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses


3. Access Microsoft's symbol server, which hosts .NET runtime symbols using a specific URL format with the BuildId

I'll need to construct the proper symbol server URLs for these libraries and attempt to download the debug information. Now I need to construct the proper URL format to download the debug symbols from Microsoft's server, using the filename and BuildId for each library. Once I have those symbols, I'll need to check what tools like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` are available to actually resolve the memory addresses.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
mkdir -p /tmp/sv-aabaf2bfa7f14b9e9bab4d28f56c4f88/symbols && cd /tmp/sv-aabaf2bfa7f14b9e9bab4d28f56c4f88/symbols

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# libmonosgen-2.0.so BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
echo "=== Trying libmonosgen-2.0.so ==="
curl -sSL -o libmonosgen-2.0.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be/libmonosgen-2.0.so" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1
curl -sSL -o libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be/_.debug" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying libSystem.Native.so ==="
curl -sSL -o libSystem.Native.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libSystem.Native.so/elf-buildid-7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213/libSystem.Native.so" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1
curl -sSL -o libSystem.Native.so.dbg "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213/_.debug" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying libSystem.Globalization.Native.so ==="
curl -sSL -o libSystem.Globalization.Native.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libSystem.Globalization.Native.so/elf-buildid-2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e/libSystem.Globalization.Native.so" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1
curl -sSL -o libSystem.Globalization.Native.so.dbg "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e/_.debug" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
I have `addr2line` and `aarch64-linux-gnu-addr2line` (since the crash is arm64). Now I need to download the debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server.

Let me try to download the symbols for each library. The standard URL pattern for Microsoft's symbol server for ELF files is:

`https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/<filename>`

or just:
`https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-<buildid>/<filename>`

Let me try both formats.

data.reasoningText:
I've successfully symbolicated the libmonosgen-2.0.so frames. The other two libraries (libSystem.Native.so and libSystem.Globalization.Native.so) had BuildIds that didn't resolve on Microsoft's symbol server, so I can't symbolicate those.

Let me present the results clearly and clean up.

data.content:
Here are the symbolicated .NET runtime frames from `libmonosgen-2.0.so`:

| Frame | PC | Function | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| #01 | `0x222098` | `ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast` | `mono/metadata/icall.c:6244` |
| #02 | `0x222234` | `ves_icall_System_ArgIterator_Setup` | `mono/metadata/icall.c:6353` |
| #03 | `0x19bac0` | `get_saved_signal_handler` | `mono/mini/mini-posix.c:140` |
| #06 | `0x19bc14` | `sampling_thread_func` | `mono/mini/mini-posix.c:640` |

**Frames #04, #07** (`libSystem.Native.so`) and **#05** (`libSystem.Globalization.Native.so`) could not be symbolicated — their BuildIds were not found on the Microsoft symbol server. These may be from a custom or pre-release .NET SDK build.

The crash itself is a **SIGSEGV (null pointer dereference)** triggered during `Environment.FailFast`, with the signal handler chain involving Mono's POSIX signal handling infrastructure.
